description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to run a quick security scan on a single application log file that already exists on the system.

Initial state
-------------
Directory: /home/user/devops  
File: /home/user/devops/server.log (already present)

Objective
---------
1. Isolate all “SECURITY ALERT” lines from /home/user/devops/server.log and write them—verbatim and in their original order—to a new file named  
   /home/user/devops/security_alerts.log

2. From those same “SECURITY ALERT” lines, extract the offending source IP addresses and write a second file named  
   /home/user/devops/blacklist.txt  
   with the following exact format (the automated grader will compare it byte-for-byte):

   • Line 1 # Auto-generated blacklist  
   • Line 2 <blank line>  
   • Line 3 … n one unique IPv4 address per line, sorted in ascending numeric order.

   Example layout (do not hard-code the IPs; generate them from the log):
   # Auto-generated blacklist

   10.0.0.5  
   203.0.113.45

3. Ensure both new files reside directly inside /home/user/devops and are world-readable (chmod 644).

Constraints
-----------
• You only need 3–4 terminal commands to finish the job (think grep/awk/sort/uniq, redirection, etc.).  
• Do not modify /home/user/devops/server.log.  
• Do not create any extra files in /home/user/devops; only the two specified files are allowed.  
• Keep command output to the console concise—textual confirmation is fine, but no interactive prompts.

Verification
------------
The automated test will check:

A. /home/user/devops/security_alerts.log exists and contains every line from server.log that begins with any text and then the exact string “SECURITY ALERT”.  
B. /home/user/devops/blacklist.txt exists, is formatted exactly as described, and contains a unique, numerically-sorted list of the IPs that appear after “ip=” on those same “SECURITY ALERT” lines.  
C. Both new files have permission mode 644.

Hint
----
Pipelines of grep → awk → sort → uniq with > redirects will get the job done in the required 3–4 commands.
truth:
Pre-existing file
-----------------
Path: /home/user/devops/server.log  
Permissions: 644  
Contents (7 lines total):

2024-05-15 10:01:22 INFO User login succeeded: user=alice ip=192.168.1.10  
2024-05-15 10:05:03 SECURITY ALERT Failed password attempt: user=root ip=203.0.113.45  
2024-05-15 10:07:45 ERROR Database connection timeout  
2024-05-15 10:12:11 SECURITY ALERT Multiple invalid login attempts: user=bob ip=198.51.100.23  
2024-05-15 10:15:55 INFO Cron job finished  
2024-05-15 10:17:20 SECURITY ALERT Failed password attempt: user=carol ip=203.0.113.45  
2024-05-15 10:20:00 INFO Backup completed  

Expected files after task completion
------------------------------------
1. /home/user/devops/security_alerts.log (permissions 644)  
   Exact content (3 lines, preserved order):

2024-05-15 10:05:03 SECURITY ALERT Failed password attempt: user=root ip=203.0.113.45  
2024-05-15 10:12:11 SECURITY ALERT Multiple invalid login attempts: user=bob ip=198.51.100.23  
2024-05-15 10:17:20 SECURITY ALERT Failed password attempt: user=carol ip=203.0.113.45  

2. /home/user/devops/blacklist.txt (permissions 644)  
   Exact content (5 lines):

# Auto-generated blacklist

198.51.100.23  
203.0.113.45
name: task_000000_26dcbc10
